title: Methods marked pure must not mutate observable state
messageFormat: Method '{0}' is marked pure but writes to shared state; remove the side effect or remove the attribute.
summary: Applying [Pure] tells callers and tooling that a method computes a result without changing any observable state, which makes it safe to elide, reorder, or cache. A pure method that assigns to a field or property of its type, or to static state, breaks that promise: callers that rely on the annotation will silently drop the side effect and behave incorrectly.
Either remove the mutation so the method honours its contract, or remove the [Pure] attribute so the side effect is visible to everyone reading the signature.
